{"step_by_step_thinking": "Daridorexant is a dual orexin receptor antagonist (DORA), a class of medications that block the binding of orexin A and orexin B to OX1 and OX2 receptors, which are involved in promoting wakefulness. The mechanism of action of these antagonists counteracts orexin-mediated nighttime awakenings, thereby helping to improve sleep onset and maintenance. Multiple documents confirm that dual orexin receptor antagonists, including suvorexant, lemborexant, and daridorexant, are used in the treatment of insomnia. Specifically, Document [0] states that these medications block orexin signaling and are used to treat insomnia. Document [6] notes that lemborexant, a DORA, is used in the management of insomnia. Document [12] confirms that lemborexant is in the dual orexin antagonist class and is used for insomnia. Document [26] includes suvorexant (a DORA) in the pharmacological treatment of insomnia, and since daridorexant is in the same class, it is reasonable to infer that it is also effective for insomnia based on the established efficacy of this class. Although the documents do not explicitly state daridorexant's efficacy, the class-wide evidence and mechanism of action strongly support its use. Therefore, based on the pharmacological class and established use of similar agents, daridorexant is effective for insomnia.", "answer_choice": "A"}
